Container.Name: CalendarIT
Container.Overview: A lightweight, modern, self-hosted calendar. Make events (one-off and recurring), get email reminders, colour-code your events, import/export iCalendar (.ics), and sync to your phone with any CalDAV client. It does what you want — and just that.
Container.Description: CalendarIT — a lightweight, self-hosted calendar (React web UI + ASP.NET Core REST API). Recurring events (RRULE) with correct time zones and DST, per-event colours, email reminders, and iCalendar (.ics) import/export. This single-container build runs the React UI behind nginx and the API together in one image, storing data in a built-in SQLite database (or point it at your own Postgres). Mount /data to your appdata share so your events and uploaded avatars survive container updates. A JWT signing key is auto-generated and persisted under /data on first run; set your own JWT Signing Key to override.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the CalendarIT web UI. Browse to http://[Unraid-IP]:[this port].

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Host folder where CalendarIT stores its SQLite database, uploaded avatars, and the auto-generated JWT signing key. Point this at your appdata share to persist data across updates.

Config.Name: JWT Signing Key
Config.Description: Optional. Secret (at least 32 characters) used to sign login tokens. LEAVE EMPTY to auto-generate one and persist it under /data (recommended). Set your own only if you need a fixed key across multiple instances; changing it later signs everyone out.

Config.Name: Environment
Config.Description: Production (default) or Development. Development also exposes the OpenAPI document at /openapi/v1.json.

Config.Name: Database Provider
Config.Description: Sqlite (built-in, default — recommended for most self-hosters) or Postgres. If set to Postgres, also fill in the Postgres Connection string.

Config.Name: Postgres Connection
Config.Description: Optional. Full Npgsql connection string, required only when Database Provider is Postgres. Example: Host=192.168.1.10;Port=5432;Database=calendarit;Username=calendarit;Password=secret. Ignored while Database Provider is Sqlite.

Config.Name: SMTP Host
Config.Description: Optional. SMTP server for email reminders. LEAVE EMPTY to run without email — reminders then land in the container log instead. Setting this enables email delivery (then also set SMTP From).

Config.Name: SMTP Port
Config.Description: SMTP port — 587 for STARTTLS (default), 465 for implicit TLS (also set Use SSL to true). Ignored while SMTP Host is empty.

Config.Name: SMTP Username
Config.Description: Optional SMTP login username. Leave empty for an unauthenticated relay. Ignored while SMTP Host is empty.

Config.Name: SMTP Password
Config.Description: Optional SMTP login password, paired with SMTP Username. Ignored while SMTP Host is empty.

Config.Name: SMTP From
Config.Description: From address for outgoing email, e.g. calendarit@example.com. Required once SMTP Host is set; ignored while SMTP Host is empty.

Config.Name: SMTP Use SSL
Config.Description: false = STARTTLS on port 587 (default); true = implicit TLS on port 465. Ignored while SMTP Host is empty.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Minimum log level written to the container log: Verbose, Debug, Information (default), Warning, Error, or Fatal.
